channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Man City preparing for Guardiola departure
item.description: Manchester City are preparing for manager Pep Guardiola to leave the club at the end of the season after a decade in charge and 17 major trophies.

item.title: Tense, tetchy but triumphant - all Arsenal eyes now on Bournemouth
item.description: All Arsenal eyes will be on Bournemouth v Man City on Tuesday with the Gunners agonisingly close to ending their 22-year wait for a Premier League title.

item.title: Guardiola's legacy of genius will echo through all ages for years to come
item.description: As Manchester City prepare for Pep Guardiola's exit, Phil McNulty reflects on how he has changed football in England.

item.title: 'Miles away' - Havertz 'lucky' to avoid red card
item.description: Kai Havertz takes Arsenal closer to winning their first Premier League title in 22 years, but nearly goes from hero to villain as he escapes a red card.

item.title: Clarke's key questions for Scotland World Cup squad
item.description: Steve Clarke names his Scotland squad for the World Cup on Tuesday and while the head coach has a largely settled group, BBC Sport Scotland takes a look at potential bolters.

item.title: Neymar included in Brazil's 26-man World Cup squad
item.description: Forward Neymar is included in head coach Carlo Ancelotti's 26-man Brazil squad for this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Big motivation if people want Spurs down - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham boss Roberto de Zerbi says rival fans willing the club to be relegated from the Premier League acts as a "big motivation" to stay up.

item.title: PFA charity let down players, says regulator
item.description: Conflicts of interest and "serious mismanagement" a footballers' charity put funds to help players in need at risk, a Charity Commission inquiry says.

item.title: PGMO admits handball error on Man Utd goal v Forest
item.description: Manchester United's second goal in Sunday's Premier League win over Nottingham Forest should have been disallowed for handball, the referees' body has admitted.

item.title: Man City's Shaw wins WSL Player of the Season
item.description: Manchester City striker Khadija Shaw wins the WSL Football's Player of the Season and Golden Boot awards at a ceremony in London.

item.title: Leeds promise bans over homophobic chants
item.description: Leeds United say fans found to have been responsible for homophobic chanting during their Premier League game against Brighton will face "lengthy stadium bans".

item.title: Arsenal one step closer to title after hard-fought win over Burnley
item.description: Arsenal take a huge step towards becoming Premier League champions as Kai Havertz's goal is enough to beat relegated Burnley at Emirates Stadium.

item.title: Havertz should have been sent off - Jackson
item.description: Burnley interim manager Mike Jackson is pleased with the way his side kept themselves in the game in their 1-0 defeat at Arsenal and is unhappy Kai Havertz was not sent off in the second half.

item.title: We played some of our best football of season in first half - Arteta
item.description: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta feels his side played some of their "best football" of the season in their hard-fought 1-0 win over Burnley which edges them closer to their first Premier League title for 22 years.

item.title: Should Cunha's goal have been disallowed?
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Mark Schwarzer and Dion Dublin believe Bryan Mbeumo using his arm to trap the ball to his side to help better control Diego Dalot's cross in the build-up to Matheus Cunha's goal, gave Manchester United an "unfair advantage" in their 3-2 win over Nottingham Forest in the Premier League.

item.title: If I was Slot I'd have Salah nowhere near the stadium - Rooney
item.description: After Mohamed Salah's social media post questioning Liverpool's style of play, Wayne Rooney says he would have the forward "nowhere near the stadium" for their final Premier League match.

item.title: Selfish Salah should be dropped from Anfield finale - Rooney
item.description: Mohamed Salah should be dropped from Liverpool's season finale for "selfish" criticism of the club, says Wayne Rooney.

item.title: West Ham relegation may cost London taxpayers £2.5m
item.description: London taxpayers may have to pay an extra £2.5m if West Ham are relegated from the Premier League, because of the club's lease agreement for London Stadium.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Every nation's squad as they are announced
item.description: Every squad for the 2026 World Cup in the United States, Canada and Mexico as they are announced.

item.title: 'I was screaming' - footballer stabbed seven times recalls mass knife attack
item.description: Footballer Jonathan Gjoshe was among the 11 passengers seriously injured in a knife attack on a train as it travelled through Cambridgeshire last November.

item.title: Mourinho wants Rashford at Real Madrid - Tuesday gossip
item.description: Jose Mourinho wants Marcus Rashford at Real Madrid, Everton and Aston Villa interested in William Osula, Levi Colwill not currently in talks over an improved Chelsea contract, plus more.

item.title: Giroud, Azpilicueta and McCarthy join BBC World Cup team
item.description: BBC Sport announces its plans for the 2026 Fifa World Cup next month including coverage of all 104 matches in the United States, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: Stories, scheduling & spectacle - five takeaways from 2025-26 WSL
item.description: With the 2025-26 season in the books, BBC Sport answers five pertinent questions from the Women's Super League.

item.title: Croatia name Modric, 40, in World Cup squad
item.description: Veteran midfielder Luka Modric is named in Croatia's squad for the World Cup and is expected to recover from a fractured cheekbone in time for the tournament.

item.title: Europe 'best way' to end Bournemouth stay - Iraola
item.description: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola says finishing in a European place would be the "best way" to finish his time at the club.

item.title: No Celtic future talks before cup final for O'Neill
item.description: Martin O'Neill does not expect there to be discussion about the Celtic manager's position before Saturday's Scottish Cup final, and doubts he would "have the energy" to continue.

item.title: How overperforming Villa returned to Champions League
item.description: Eight places better off than their expected position, Aston Villa are the highest overperforming team in the Premier League.

item.title: Town of 13,000 people spawns a Bundesliga team
item.description: Elversberg will become one of the smallest clubs ever to play in the Bundesliga after securing a first promotion to Germany's top flight.

item.title: Fans' fury as West Ham on brink of drop - how have they got here?
item.description: It was less than three years ago that West Ham were lifting a European trophy in Prague. But they are now on the verge of relegation from Premier League.

item.title: Carvajal to leave Real Madrid after 23 years
item.description: Real Madrid captain Dani Carvajal will leave the club at the end of this season after two spells at the Santiago Bernabeu covering 23 years.

item.title: Neymar furious as he is wrongly substituted
item.description: Neymar was involved in a furious row with officials after Santos claimed the forward had been wrongly substituted in their 3-0 defeat by Coritiba - a day before Brazil announce their World Cup squad.

item.title: Austria include Arnautovic in World Cup squad
item.description: Former West Ham and Stoke striker Marko Arnautovic is included in Austria's squad for their first appearance at a World Cup in 28 years.

item.title: Ex-Everton director loses Russian sanctions challenge
item.description: Sarvar Ismailov was sanctioned in 2022 due to his billionaire uncle's ties to Vladimir Putin.

item.title: So many positives for Hearts despite final day pain - McInnes
item.description: Head coach Derek McInnes believes Heart of Midlothian can use this season as a springboard once they recover from the hurt of the final day.

item.title: Former De Zerbi assistant becomes Ukraine manager
item.description: Andrea Maldera, a former assistant manager of Tottenham boss Roberto De Zerbi, has been appointed Ukraine head coach.

item.title: Osula stars as Newcastle increase West Ham's relegation worries
item.description: William Osula scores twice as Newcastle soundly defeat West Ham United 3-1, to leave Nuno Espirito Santo's side on the brink of relegation from the Premier League.

item.title: Fernandes equals assist record as Man Utd beat Forest
item.description: Bruno Fernandes earns his 20th Premier League assist of the season, equalling the record set by Kevin De Bruyne and Thierry Henry, as Manchester United defeat Nottingham Forest 3-2 in their final home game of the campaign at Old Trafford.

item.title: Sunderland keep Europe hopes alive with win at Everton
item.description: Sunderland come from behind to defeat Everton 3-1 at the Hill Dickinson Stadium, moving up to ninth in the Premier League.

item.title: Outtara double rescues home draw for Brentford against Palace
item.description: Dango Outtara scores twice to earn Brentford a 2-2 draw at home to Crystal Palace in the Premier League, as Keith Andrews' side stay in the hunt for European football.

item.title: Why Salah statement shows how Liverpool have become disjointed
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Mark Schwarzer and Dion Dublin believe Mohamed Salah's comments on social media regarding Liverpool's style of play under Arne Slot show there is a lack of happiness and harmony within the squad.

item.title: Late Leeds winner deals blow to Brighton's Europa League hopes
item.description: Dominic Calvert-Lewin's 96th-minute goal gives Leeds United a 1-0 win over Brighton & Hove Albion at Elland Road, as Fabian Hurzeler's side stay in 7th heading into the final week of the Premier League season.

item.title: How 'poor' West Ham were 'torn apart' by Newcastle
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Mark Schwarzer and Dion Dublin analyse how Newcastle United were able to exploit large spaces between West Ham's back three, in their 3-1 win at St James' Park in the Premier League.

item.title: Fulham held to draw at bottom side Wolves
item.description: Fulham's fading hopes of European qualification suffered a terminal blow as they drew against Wolves in the Premier League.

item.title: Will Wharton be in England's World Cup squad?
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Mark Schwarzer and Dion Dublin analyse Adam Wharton's performance in Crystal Palace's 2-2 draw with Brentford and argue that the England midfielder could cement his place in Thomas Tuchel's World Cup squad, if he can have an impressive performance in the UEFA Conference League final.

item.title: Shamrock's Lopes makes Cape Verde's World Cup squad
item.description: Shamrock Rovers centre-back Roberto Lopes is named in Cape Verde's 26-man squad for this summer's World Cup.

item.title: O'Neill rejects 'nonsense' criticism of pitch invasion
item.description: Celtic manager Martin O'Neill says it is "nonsense" to suggest the pitch invasion that followed Saturday's dramatic Scottish Premiership title win embarrassed the game.

item.title: Nine teams, four spots - Premier League set for European scramble
item.description: The Premier League title and relegation fights might be dominating the headlines, but the race for Europe is proving to be an equally chaotic and enthralling battle this season.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Michael Jackson and Spider-Man – Premier League celebrations take a turn
item.description: After brief appearances by the King of Pop and the web-slinging superhero on Sunday, we take at look at some of the Premier League's most memorable celebrations.

item.title: Who is pushing for late England World Cup call-up?
item.description: With Thomas Tuchel naming his England squad for the World Cup on Friday, which players have made late pushes to be included?

item.title: Tipped for relegation to title winners - AGF's 'King Arthur' moment
item.description: Aarhus GF were tipped for relegation from the Danish Superliga this season. Instead, they will lift the championship trophy for the first time in 40 years this Sunday.

item.title: Another handball 'mess' - is it time to change law?
item.description: The decision to allow Matheus Cunha's goal to stand, following an "accidental" handball by Bryan Mbeumo in the build-up, has baffled pundits, players and fans.

item.title: What Man City's FA Cup win means for Premier League places in Europe
item.description: The top eight in the Premier League will now qualify for Europe - and sixth could yet end up in the Champions League. Here's how.

item.title: Truth stranger than fiction as O'Neill leads Celtic from hostility to happiness
item.description: Martin O'Neill said Celtic would have to win seven out of seven in the run-in to win the Scottish Premiership - here is how they did it.

item.title: Three Lions - the football anthem that united a nation
item.description: BBC Sport speaks to Three Lions co-creator David Baddiel about the song that united a nation.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: What does Premiership climax mean for European places?
item.description: What does Saturday's dramatic and chaotic climax to the Scottish Premiership top-six mean for European football next season?

item.title: All you need to know about the Europa League final
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at all you need to know before this year's Europa League final.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: Title party fanzones not possible due to cost, says council
item.description: Glasgow's Trongate area saw clashes between Celtic fans and police on Saturday, after the Parkhead side dramatically won the SPFL.

item.title: Rangers set for 'summer of change' as Cavenagh backs Rohl
item.description: Rangers chairman Andrew Cavenagh backs manager Danny Rohl and says a "summer of substantial change" will happen at Ibrox after the failings of this season.

item.title: Earps close to WSL return as PSG exit nears
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps is close to a return to the Women’s Super League, with her exit from Paris St-Germain edging closer.

item.title: 'They always deliver' - Man City savour 'magical' title
item.description: For the first time in seven seasons, the ribbons on the Women's Super League trophy are a different shade of blue after Manchester City's "magical" triumph.

item.title: Why summers of change beckon for WSL giants
item.description: With players departing and improved seasons needed, there could be summers of change for Arsenal and Chelsea.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
